snippet: Applications for personal leave must be supported by a required document, Where a required document is provided for personal leave (carer’s) it must state that the person requiring care and support is suffering from an illness which requires care by another or requires care or support due to an unexpected emergency. Where an application for personal leave is granted and a required document is required and is not provided, the absence will be without pay. Where the delegate has a reasonable belief that the employee was unfit for duty and was unable to obtain a required document, the delegate may approve personal leave with pay. Where there is cause to doubt the cause of an illness or injury or the reason for absence the delegate may refer any required document to a medical practitioner for report or contact the Medical Advisory Service for advice.
